message:
Improve show command table layout

The title column now expands to fill available width, component labels appear
inline after the title in dimmed green, and the column order is adjusted to
show PR before Type. Empty PR values display as a dimmed dash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+title: Enhanced table layout for show command
+type: change
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2026-01-09T10:27:16.163588Z
+---
+
+The title column now expands to fill available terminal width, ensuring optimal use of screen space. Component labels appear inline after the title in a dimmed green style, replacing the separate component column. The column order has been adjusted to show PR numbers before the entry type, and the column header changed from "PRs" to "PR". Empty PR values display as a dimmed dash for visual consistency.

@@ -55,6 +55,7 @@
     "IdentifierResolution",
     "OverflowMethod",
     "JustifyMethod",
+    "create_table",
     "_print_renderable",
     "_entries_table_layout",
     "_ellipsis_cell",
@@ -79,6 +80,22 @@
 ]
 
 
+def create_table(expand: bool = False, **kwargs) -> Table:
+    """Create a borderless table with consistent styling.
+
+    This is the standard table style used across the CLI. It has no borders,
+    relying on spacing and optional manual divider columns for visual separation.
+
+    Args:
+        expand: Whether the table should expand to fill available width.
+        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to rich.table.Table
+
+    Returns:
+        A configured Table instance
+    """
+    return Table(box=None, show_lines=False, expand=expand, pad_edge=False, **kwargs)
+
+
 IdentifierKind = Literal["row", "entry", "release", "unreleased"]
